canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, Natural Instructions is characterized as a dataset for evaluation. Its stated purpose is to assess a model's ability to follow instructions given in natural language. The definition specifies that this evaluation spans a wide range of tasks. The single paper surveyed uses this dataset to test the effects of different methods on generation tasks, illustrating its application in an experimental context.
papers: Ferret: Federated Full-Parameter Tuning at Scale for Large Language Models
On Teacher Hacking in Language Model Distillation
What Limits Bidirectional Model’s Generative Capabilities? A Uni-Bi-Directional Mixture-of-Expert Method For Bidirectional Fine-tuning

definitions.text: A dataset for evaluating a model's ability to follow natural language instructions across a wide range of tasks.
definitions.paper: What Limits Bidirectional Model’s Generative Capabilities? A Uni-Bi-Directional Mixture-of-Expert Method For Bidirectional Fine-tuning
